assertions.description: The Fetch API is exposed; Compression Dictionary Transport rides over fetch responses that include the Use-As-Dictionary header.
The page is running in a secure context. Compression Dictionary Transport is gated to secure contexts.
Headers exposes get/set/delete so userland can inspect the Use-As-Dictionary response header including the new ttl parameter.
A fetch Request can be constructed and a Request#headers surface exists so the browser-supplied Available-Dictionary request header surface is observable in principle.
Headers preserves dcb/dcz content-coding tokens used by Compression Dictionary Transport (the wire format the ttl scopes the dictionary's lifetime for).
